market_context:
Cohort size: 50 listings scraped in the same market.
Median nightly rate: £104.90
Median 90-day occupancy: 48.9%
Median photo count: 23

title_variants: Historic Temple Chambers Apt w/ Mood Lighting
analysis.rewritten_desc:
Welcome to a stylish, fully private one‑bedroom apartment tucked in Sheffield’s historic Cathedral Quarter. Housed in the beautifully restored Temple Chambers (formerly Synagogue Chambers), the flat sits on a quiet, flagstone pedestrian lane just a 10‑minute walk from the train station. 

The second‑floor unit is bright and airy by day, and comes alive after dark with customizable mood lighting that lets you set the ambience to match your mood. The open‑plan living, dining and kitchen area is framed by reconfigurable curtains, so you can enjoy a spacious lounge or a cosy, separated dining nook. 

Features include a comfortable bedroom with ample wardrobe space, a modern bathroom, a 65‑inch smart TV, high‑speed Wi‑Fi, and a fully equipped kitchen (induction cooktop, oven, microwave, kettle, fridge). Laundry is simple with an in‑unit washing machine, iron, ironing board, clothes horse and vacuum cleaner. Additional conveniences are a digital building entry, shoe storage in the hallway, and dedicated workspace. 

Perfect for couples or small families seeking a blend of historic charm and contemporary comforts, this apartment offers a unique stay in the heart of Sheffield while keeping you completely private and relaxed.
top_amenity_gaps: Dedicated parking or guaranteed off‑street parking
Carbon monoxide alarm for enhanced safety
analysis.photo_order_rec: Use a bright living‑room shot showcasing the colour‑changing mood lighting as the cover photo; it instantly conveys the unique ambience and open‑plan layout that set this listing apart.
key_diagnosis: Raise the nightly rate from £78.4 (25% below the cohort median of £104.90) to around £95 to capture more revenue while still staying competitively priced.
Replace the current cover image with the existing living‑room mood‑lighting photo to improve click‑through rates; the listing already has 37 photos covering all rooms.
Add a carbon monoxide alarm (currently missing) and clearly list guaranteed parking in the amenities section to close high‑value amenity gaps and boost guest confidence.
analysis.outreach_hook: Your apartment is priced 25% below the city median (£78 vs £105) yet its 90‑day occupancy sits at just 46.7%—there’s clear upside to boost bookings.
